title: Steam Machine suffers from a 'red line of death' due to GPU failure after a few minutes of use
summary: Several users report that the Steam Machine console freezes with a red line on the screen due to a GPU failure, reminiscent of the Xbox 360's red ring of death.

title: Intel solves 18A node performance issues and reaches 30,000 wafers per month
summary: Intel has resolved the performance issues of its 18A manufacturing process, achieving production of 30,000 wafers per month across its two factories.

title: Sony's controversial decision to eliminate physical format on PlayStation sparks reactions and criticism
summary: The Video Game History Foundation warns about the impact of the end of the physical format, while players try to avoid it and GitHub launches CD-ROMs in protest.

title: Valve opens source code for Steam Machine screen and interface so users can build their own consoles
summary: Valve has published on GitLab the code for the Inkterface interface and the e-ink screen of the Steam Machine, allowing enthusiasts to create their own versions.

title: Intel confirms price increases on select processors, including Xeon and Core Ultra
summary: Intel has raised prices on some consumer and server processors due to supply costs and demand, with increases of over $1,000 on some Xeons.

title: Details leaked of Intel Nova Lake-S processors with 22 cores and cache optimized for gaming
summary: Future Intel Nova Lake-S processors will include 22-core models with a special cache to improve gaming performance, according to rumors.

title: DRAM price expected to rise up to 20% in Q3 2026 due to AI demand
summary: The growing demand for memory for AI data centers will drive a DRAM price increase of between 15% and 20% in the third quarter of 2026.

title: Anthropic develops its own AI chip in collaboration with Samsung
summary: AI company Anthropic is working on a custom AI processor with Samsung to strengthen its position in the sector.

title: Safari launches MCP server for automated browser debugging
summary: Safari has introduced a server based on the Model Context Protocol that allows developers to debug the browser automatically.

title: Zuckerberg admits that AI agent development at Meta is slower than expected
summary: Mark Zuckerberg has told Meta employees that the AI agents are not progressing at the expected pace.

title: MEP investigating espionage hacked with Pegasus software
summary: The phone of a European Parliament member who was part of an investigation into spyware abuses was infected with Pegasus malware.

title: Amazon Leo surpasses 390 satellites and prepares to compete with Starlink
summary: Amazon's satellite internet project, Leo, has reached more than 390 satellites in orbit, enough to start service and rival Starlink.

title: Fable 5 development faces technical and ethical challenges with AI use
summary: The Fable 5 studio has reduced costs by converting code into images for OCR, but Anthropic has blocked the use of its AI to write game code.

title: Anthropic expands Claude's capabilities with Code Artifacts and lab access
summary: Anthropic has made Claude Code Artifacts available to paying users and integrated Claude into labs so it can write and execute code.

title: Meta optimizes AI costs by selling compute capacity and reusing DDR4 memory in DDR5 servers
summary: Meta has started selling AI compute capacity to third parties and developed a CXL chip to reuse DDR4 memory in new DDR5 servers, thus reducing hardware costs.

title: Intel revives 13th and 14th Gen processors in China after stability issues
summary: Intel has put 13th and 14th generation Core processors on sale again in the Chinese market, while a study suggests consumer confidence has been affected by the stability issues.

title: Microsoft accelerates its AI strategy with Windows Project Aion and the Frontier Company program
summary: Microsoft plans an AI-focused Windows operating system called Project Aion, and has launched a $2.5 billion program to integrate 6,000 AI engineers into client companies.

title: Xbox develops feature to convert physical games to digital while its lack of strategy is criticized
summary: Xbox is working on a tool that allows converting physical game discs into digital copies, while analysts point out that the company lacks a clear strategy after layoffs and closures.

title: New high-speed SSDs: Samsung 990 reaches 7,250 MB/s and Kioxia promises a big leap with its new memory
summary: Samsung has introduced its 990 PCIe 4.0 SSD with read speeds of up to 7,250 MB/s, while Kioxia is developing new memory that could revolutionize SSD performance.

title: Google loses appeal to EU and must pay €4.1 billion for Android monopoly
summary: The Court of Justice of the European Union has confirmed the €4.1 billion fine on Google for abusing its dominant position with Android.
